heroTitle: A stronger, more democratic union for every member.
heroLede: We are running to build a CUPE 3906 that listens, communicates clearly, and fights for better working conditions, stronger representation, and a more connected union community. Meet our team and learn why each candidate is running.
introText: Our slate brings together candidates from across departments who are committed to advocacy, accessibility, accountability, and collective action. Click on any candidate to learn more about their background, priorities, and vision for the union.
footerText: Union for Everyone | CUPE 3906 election campaign

candidates.name: Avery Bennett
candidates.statement: I am running for President because I believe our union is strongest when every worker feels heard, respected, and represented. I want to strengthen member outreach, improve transparency in decision-making, and make sure our bargaining priorities reflect the daily realities of graduate workers across campus.

candidates.name: Sofia Malik
candidates.statement: I am running for Vice-President to help build a more responsive and connected union. My focus is on communication, organizing, and making sure members can easily understand how to get support, raise concerns, and participate in union life.

candidates.name: Jordan Chen
candidates.statement: I am running for Secretary-Treasurer because careful stewardship of union resources matters. I want members to feel confident that finances are managed responsibly, updates are easy to follow, and budgeting reflects the priorities we set together.

candidates.name: Rishav Jaiswal
candidates.statement: I am running for Recording Secretary because good records create accountability. I want meeting notes, decisions, and follow-up actions to be clear, timely, and accessible so members can stay informed and engaged.

candidates.name: Daniel Okoye
candidates.statement: I am running for Chief Steward to strengthen workplace support across departments. Members deserve responsive representation, clear grievance guidance, and a union culture where nobody feels alone when problems arise.

candidates.name: Mina Haddad
candidates.statement: I am running for Equity Officer because inclusion has to be active, not symbolic. I want to support anti-oppression work, improve access to union spaces, and make sure our campaigns reflect the needs of marginalized members.

candidates.name: Ethan Rodrigues
candidates.statement: I am running for Communications Officer because members need updates that are timely, clear, and welcoming. I want to improve outreach through better messaging, stronger visual materials, and easier access to key information.

candidates.name: Fatima Noor
candidates.statement: I am running for Health and Safety Officer because healthy workplaces are a union issue. I want to elevate members' concerns around workload, stress, and physical safety while pushing for more proactive protections.

candidates.name: Lucas Pereira
candidates.statement: I am running for Social Events Coordinator because community matters. I want to help create events that are accessible, affordable, and meaningful so members can build relationships beyond moments of crisis.

candidates.name: Harpreet Gill
candidates.statement: I am running for Grievance Officer because workers need steady support when navigating difficult situations. I want to make the grievance process more approachable and ensure members know what options are available to them.

candidates.name: Nadia El-Sayed
candidates.statement: I am running for International Members Representative because international students and workers face distinct barriers that deserve focused advocacy. I want to improve information-sharing, support, and solidarity across the local.

candidates.name: Noah Sinclair
candidates.statement: I am running for Unit 1 Representative to bring everyday workplace concerns forward quickly and consistently. I want to listen well, report back clearly, and help members feel more connected to union decision-making.

candidates.name: Leila Farah
candidates.statement: I am running for Unit 2 Representative because contract academic workers deserve strong, visible advocacy. I want to focus on equity, workplace respect, and practical organizing that improves conditions across the unit.
